Class ResolveTypeReference

java.lang.Object
recoder.kit.Transformation
recoder.kit.TwoPassTransformation
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.removegenerics.GenericResolutionTransformation
de.uka.ilkd.key.util.removegenerics.ResolveTypeReference
public class ResolveTypeReference extends GenericResolutionTransformation
Handle a type reference in the generic deletion process. If the type reference references a type avar or an array over tv, it must be replaced. if the type var is ("extends"-) bounded than with the first boundary(!) otherwise with java.lang.Object.

  • Constructor Details

    • ResolveTypeReference

      public ResolveTypeReference(TypeReference reference, CrossReferenceServiceConfiguration sc)

  • Method Details

    • analyze

      public ProblemReport analyze()
      Description copied from class: TwoPassTransformation
      Prepares all necessary information for the syntactic transformation and checks the transformation requirements. This method may call any queries but may not change the model directly (the model might be augmented by automatic reload, however). This default implementation sets and returns Transformation.NO_PROBLEM.
      Overrides:
      analyze in class TwoPassTransformation
      Returns:
      a problem report, an instance of NoProblemif all requirements have been met.

    • transform

      public void transform()
      Description copied from class: TwoPassTransformation
      Performs the syntactic transformation. This method may not call any query that may lead to a model update. If this transformation is visible, change reports are send to the change history. The default implementation will check if the problem report has been initialized, and report the begin of the transformation to the change history if this transformation is visible. Redefined methods should call this method via super.transform() as the first action.
      Overrides:
      transform in class TwoPassTransformation
      See Also:
      • TwoPassTransformation.analyze()
      • Transformation.isVisible()
      • ChangeHistory.begin(Transformation)
